name: Hackathon: Second Brain / AI Knowledge Bot
objective: Build function-specific AI 'second brain' bots that allow team members to query meeting transcripts, Granola notes, and Slack conversations to quickly access decisions, context, and perspectives from senior leaders and cross-functional meetings — reducing information bottlenecks and the need to interrupt busy team members for status updates.

audience.description: Internal B2B SaaS marketing team members who need access to decisions and context from meetings they did not attend. Primary users are IC-level and mid-level team members querying upward; initial focus on brand marketing function.
segments: Brand marketing team members (e.g. users who would query a 'KimBot' or brand marketing Slack channel bot)
Senior-level VP and executive stakeholders whose meeting content feeds the bot (e.g. Kim)
Any function that adopts a dedicated bot channel (e.g. 'Lizzy bar', 'brand marketing channel')

variants: Stop asking people for updates they've already discussed — query the bot first
Get clarity on where decisions have landed without waiting for a person to be free
Ask the bot before interrupting Kim or Lizzy — it's step one before going to the actual person
Organized by team function (e.g. brand, PMM) rather than individual personality for broader trust and utility
Baked-in memory includes team charters, strategic pillars, and principles (e.g. Rashida's PMM charter)
The bot knows when to say 'I don't have that information — you should speak to Kim directly'
Proper guardrails on sensitive information; bot understands what to share vs. escalate
Ethics and safety principles embedded in bot behavior
Low-tech entry point via Slack channel — accessible to all levels of the team
Bot replies in-thread so conversations stay organized
Works like a triage layer before escalating to a human

messaging.tone: Practical, straightforward, internally focused — clarity and efficiency over formality

channels.strategy: Deploy function-specific bot channels (e.g. a brand marketing Slack channel, a 'Lizzy bar' channel) where team members can ask the bot questions in natural language and receive threaded replies. Claude/AI sends daily or weekly summaries per function/VP. Each channel is scoped to its function and not cross-visible.
assets: Function-specific Slack bot channels (e.g. brand marketing channel)
Daily/weekly automated summary posts per VP or function

channels.strategy: Ingest Granola meeting transcripts and notes as the primary data source for the bot's knowledge base, enabling queries against recorded decisions and discussions.
assets: Auto-ingested notes from relevant cross-functional meetings

channels.strategy: Pull relevant Slack channel conversations (ongoing projects, relevant threads) as a secondary data source for the bot's memory.

concepts: Function-specific Slack bot ('KimBot' for brand marketing, 'Lizzy bar' for Lizzy's team) that answers clarifying questions about decisions and meeting outcomes
Memory layer baked with team charters and strategic pillars (e.g. Rashida's PMM charter) so the bot answers in the context of team principles
Triage model: bot either answers the question or explicitly tells the user to go speak to the relevant person directly
Modular structure so the same bot infrastructure can be replicated across functions (brand, PMM, etc.)
assets: PMM charter (authored by Rashida) — to be embedded in bot memory

operations.timeline: FY27 brand campaign referenced as near-term use case; hackathon context implies short-term prototype
operations.campaign_name: Hackathon: Second Brain AI Bot by Function
requirements: Ingest Granola meeting transcripts per function/VP
Build memory layer including team charters and strategic pillars (e.g. Rashida's PMM charter)
Deploy as function-scoped Slack bot channels (not cross-visible by default)
Bot must know when to answer vs. when to redirect user to speak to the person directly
Implement ethics and safety guardrails on information sharing
Determine which meeting types should always be excluded from bot ingestion
Support simple natural-language Q&A with threaded replies in Slack
Modular structure replicable across functions (brand, PMM, Lizzy's team, etc.)
Consider co-worker/skill infrastructure for knowing where to look for information

issues: Ethics and safety principles not yet defined — need explicit guardrails on what information the bot can and cannot share
No decision yet on which meeting types should be excluded from ingestion
Access controls not defined — who can query which function's bot
Guardrails for when bot should defer to a human not yet implemented
Need to consult with a relevant stakeholder (e.g. Kim, Rashida, Lizzy) on use cases and acceptable queries before build
Privacy implications of ingesting meeting transcripts and Slack conversations not reviewed
